name: CARDIAC PARAGANGLIOMA
definition: extremely rare, usually benign sporadic neoplasm arising from intrinsic cardiac sympathetic

demographics.Path: 2-14 cm large encapsulated / poorly circumscribed and infiltrative highly vascular
demographics.Histo: monomorphic tumor composed of nests of paraganglial cells (= "Zellballen")
demographics.Location: posterior wall of left atrium > roof of left atrium > atrial cavity > interatrial septum >

dominant_finding: central area of low attenuation (in 50%) from necrosis
distribution: posterior wall of left atrium > roof of left atrium > atrial cavity > interatrial septum >
discriminating_features: markedly enhancing mass adherent to / involving left atrium / anterior to aortic root
central area of low attenuation (in 50%) from necrosis
isoattenuating to cardiac structures (may be missed)

CXR: middle mediastinal mass splaying carina simulating left atrial enlargement (for typically
compression of SVC, encasement of coronary arteries
for total body imaging with a sensitivity of 90%
NECT: isoattenuating to cardiac structures (may be missed)
CECT: markedly enhancing mass adherent to / involving left atrium / anterior to aortic root
central area of low attenuation (in 50%) from necrosis
MR: mass iso- / hypointense to myocardium on T1WI
